briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for June 4, 2026. Based on 129 climate-related news items collected globally today, we aim to provide an in-depth analysis of the severity of the climate crisis we currently face and the various domestic and international efforts being made to address it.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Global Warnings
sections.content: Several reports released today once again emphasize that climate change remains a severe global threat [102, 103]. Notably, news that former President Trump has begun dismantling ocean climate observation equipment, disregarding the climate crisis, raises concerns about climate science data collection [5, 100, 121]. Furthermore, the French government's decision to cut climate change adaptation funds immediately after a heatwave sends a warning that adequate responses may not be implemented [114]. Additionally, a report indicating a 2% increase in China's first-quarter carbon emissions, alongside a rise in wasted electricity despite increased renewable energy production, suggests issues with energy transition efficiency and the risk of overall emission growth [72, 109, 128]. As seen in the calculation of damages for Korean beekeepers [108, 110, 119] and the case of a Florida man relocating due to soaring insurance premiums [106], these climate changes are incurring tangible economic and social costs [117], and are having widespread impacts, such as making hurricanes more powerful [115] and exacerbating wildfire risks [124].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea's Climate Change Response Efforts and Policy Progress
sections.content: Domestically, various policy efforts to address the climate crisis are ongoing. Minister of Climate Kim Sung-hwan held a video conference with Australia's Minister for Climate Change and Energy, strengthening international cooperation [2, 9, 10]. He also announced plans to finalize amendments to the Carbon Neutrality Act, which had received a 'constitutional non-conformity' ruling, during the regular session of the National Assembly and propose strong targets [19, 55]. The government has approved an emissions trading scheme for farm heat pumps and apartment solar power, expanding community-based reductions [8]. The Ministry of Climate is accelerating the achievement of carbon neutrality goals by expanding renewable energy to 100 GW and promoting regional electricity pricing systems [34, 61, 73]. Over the past year, the Lee Jae-myung administration has laid the groundwork for a major transition to renewable energy and achieved the milestone of over 1 million electric vehicles in circulation [44, 68, 71]. Various local governments, including Yeongcheon City, Jindo County, Yeongdeungpo District, Yeoju City, and Chungju City, are encouraging climate action in their communities by operating academies for responding to abnormal climate change, providing consulting for farmers, and hosting carbon neutrality experience festivals to commemorate Environment Day [4, 6, 18, 21, 33, 37, 41, 49, 60, 66, 67].

sections.title: 🌱 Industry's Transition to Carbon Neutrality and Expansion of Renewable Energy Investment
sections.content: Domestic companies are also taking proactive steps towards achieving carbon neutrality goals. Lotte Chilsung Beverage announced that it reduced greenhouse gas emissions by 6,400 tons last year and is on track towards its '2040 Carbon Neutrality' target [15, 16, 17, 22, 31, 36, 38, 43, 45, 50, 52, 54, 56, 57, 59, 62, 74, 79, 82, 88, 89, 90, 92, 94, 95, 99]. This achievement is the result of multifaceted efforts, including process innovation, expansion of renewable energy, and introduction of electric vehicles [52, 79, 90, 99]. Furthermore, the Export-Import Bank of Korea, in partnership with Samsung Electronics, is supporting the renewable energy transition of local suppliers in Vietnam, contributing to strengthening the ESG competitiveness of global supply chains [75, 80, 83, 84, 85, 87, 91]. Ediya Coffee has begun supplying low-carbon certified milk to its nationwide stores, helping to spread a culture of carbon neutrality in daily life [32]. Boryung published its 'Sustainability Report 2026,' disclosing ESG achievements such as expanding the supply of essential medicines and declaring carbon neutrality [24, 46, 86].

sections.title: 🌍 Strengthening Climate Resilience and Future Vision of the International Community
sections.content: The international community is also exploring various approaches for adapting to climate change and envisioning the future. The Obama Presidential Center was constructed for sustainability and climate resilience, and is expected to contribute to the local community [112]. In Romania, the official launch of the Climate Change Science Advisory Council has led to an assessment that the outlook for climate action is finally 'improving' [118]. The Thomas Piketty Institute proposed ambitious plans to address global inequality and curb climate change, presenting a vision for a better future [120, 126, 127]. Interestingly, when London experienced flooding due to climate change, experts cited 'beavers' as an effective and affordable solution [122]. Furthermore, unexpected adaptation efforts are emerging, such as the fashion industry innovating office wear to combat the heat [123]. These movements demonstrate that climate change is not merely an environmental issue but is driving broader societal changes.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has demonstrated that despite the severity of the global climate crisis, governments, businesses, and civil societies worldwide are making multifaceted efforts. Addressing climate change is a long-term challenge that requires continuous attention and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.
